aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: former movie theater in Midtown Manhattan, New York City, United States
description_wiki: The Roxy Theatre was a 5,920-seat movie palace at 153 West 50th Street between 6th and 7th Avenues, just off Times Square in New York City.

description: former movie theater at 1645 Broadway, just north of Times Square in Manhattan, New York City, United States
description_wiki: The Capitol Theatre was a movie palace located at 1645 Broadway, just north of Times Square in New York City, across from the Winter Garden Theatre.

description: American drummer, composer, and bandleader (1909-1973)
description_wiki: Eugene Bertram Krupa (January 15, 1909 – October 16, 1973) was an American jazz drummer, bandleader, and composer.

description: American jazz clarinetist and bandleader (1909–1986)
description_wiki: Benjamin David Goodman (May 30, 1909 – June 13, 1986) was an American jazz clarinetist and bandleader, known as the "King of Swing".

description: American big band leader and musician
description_wiki: Thomas Francis Dorsey Jr.

description: American clarinetist, alto saxophonist, bandleader, and composer, brother of Tommy Dorsey (1904–1957)
description_wiki: James Francis Dorsey (February 29, 1904 – June 12, 1957) was an American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, composer and big band leader.

surface_forms: The Japanese just bombed Pearl Harbor!
description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: terrestrial continent located in north-western Eurasia
description_wiki: Europe is a continent located entirely in the Northern Hemisphere and mostly in the Eastern Hemisphere.

description: city in and the county seat of Passaic County, New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: Paterson (English pronunciation: /ˈpætɚsən/ ) is the largest city in and the county seat of Passaic County, in the U.S. state of New Jersey.

description: village in Rockland County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Nyack (English pronunciation: /ˈnaɪæk/ ) is a village primarily located in the town of Orangetown in Rockland County, New York, United States.

description: United States Numbered Highway in New York, United States
description_wiki: U.S. Route 9 (US 9) is a part of the United States Numbered Highway System that runs from Laurel, Delaware, to Champlain, New York.

description: village in New York, United States
description_wiki: Sleepy Hollow is a village in the town of Mount Pleasant in Westchester County, New York, United States, in the New York metropolitan area.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: president of the United States from 1929 to 1933 (1874–1964)
description_wiki: Herbert Clark Hoover (August 10, 1874 – October 20, 1964) was the 31st president of the United States, serving from 1929 to 1933.

description: school district in Passaic County, New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: The Paterson Public Schools (PPS) is a comprehensive community public school district that serves students in pre-kindergarten through twelfth grade from Paterson, in the U.S. state of New Jersey.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: United States Army base in New Jersey
description_wiki: Fort Dix, the common name for the Army Support Activity (ASA) located at Joint Base McGuire–Dix–Lakehurst, is a United States Army post.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

description: High German-derived language used by Ashkenazi Jews
